name: Ostrobothnia (Finland)

colors.symbolism: the gules field of the historic Ostrobothnian arms
the golden chief and the golden garb of the arms

description: A square flag with a golden chief bearing four red ermines over a wide red field charged with a golden garb (wheat sheaf) — the banner of the Ostrobothnia coat of arms.
history: Ostrobothnia (Pohjanmaa) became a region under Finland's 1997 administrative reform; its banner is the heraldic flag of the regional coat of arms, derived from the historic provincial arms of the broader Ostrobothnian coast.

features.role: four red ermines (2 and 2) on the golden chief
features.symbolism: the ermines are the historic emblem of Ostrobothnia, recalling the medieval fur trade of the coast.

features.role: a golden garb (wheat sheaf) on the red field
features.symbolism: the agricultural wealth of the Ostrobothnian coast.
